'奈良県高市郡明日香村真弓51', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:櫛玉命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153271}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kushitamanomikoto Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=高市郡|cat2=神社}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|27|37|N|135|47|40|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}Mayumi 51 |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 37 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 40 |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたまのみことしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|27|37|N|135|47|40|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Mayumi 51 |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 37 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 40 |祭神 = Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大くしたまのみことしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}Mayumi 51 |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|WD=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|WD=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|WD=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|WD=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|WD=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|WD=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|WD=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|WD=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}}Heibonsha ed., 'Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines: Yamato / Kii' (Heibonsha, 1997) |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (4-seat major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''Kushitamanomikoto Shrine''' (くしたまのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=enshrined|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Asuka|Q752397|qid=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Takaichi District|Q1117901|qid=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=major Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following four deities are enshrined. *Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto *Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto (Kushiakama-no-Mikoto)}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-no-Mikoto}} === History === === Historical outline === The founding period is unknown, but one theory says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Araki no Mikoto, descendant of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto (Kushitama-no-Mikoto), was granted thirty generations of land in Kose in recognition of ancestral merit, and the worship of ancestors there was the shrine's beginning. It was once called Tamatsukuri Shrine, but from early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}, and the deities were changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman|qid=Q261637}}. On December 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 23 ({{ill|lt=1890|Q7832|qid=Q7832|1=1890}}), the shrine name and deities were restored to ancient form. In Yamato Shiryo, quoting Gokōgun Jinja-ki, it says "the two deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were also invited from {{ill|lt=Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|Q11584205|qid=Q11584205|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine}} (Daidō 3)," so the deity may be {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. However, according to Takaichi District Shrine Chronicle and Nara Prefectural History, which also cite Gokōgun Jinja-ki, the enshrined Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto is said to be a child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}The original Gokōgun Jinja-ki says child of Izanagi-no-Mikoto. and father of Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, with no mention of invitation from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine. It cannot be ruled out that this is {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko-no-Kami|Q17193127|qid=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Zensho II |date=1907-02-12 |publisher=Shibunkaku}}. However, Gokōgun Jinja-ki also says "there are Yata and Hata villages in Kose-gō," "two shrines, Yatama Shrine and Hatama Shrine," and "the two distant-ancestor deities Kushitamahiko and Kushitamahime were invited from Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine and called Yatama-sha," indicating a high possibility that Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikashikiya-hime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto, and Toyotama-no-Mikoto were worshiped. Confusion may have arisen because Yata ni imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine once enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, child of Takamimusubi. Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto is considered consort of Kushitamahiko-no-Mikoto. Also, Kushitama-no-Mikoto worshiped at {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|qid=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} is Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - listed as "Kushitamanomikoto deity" === Precinct shrines === *Wakamiya (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) => no shrine building *Yasaka Shrine (deity: {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) => no shrine building === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Asuka Station|Q4811860|qid=Q4811860|1=Asuka Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yoshino Line|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888891|qid=Q134888891|1=Kushitamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine}} === External links === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22818 Kushitamanomikoto Shrine, four seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kushitamanomikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
